sections.paragraph:
ἀπερίσπαστος, ον (s. περισπάω; Polyb. 2, 20, 10; 4:18, 6; Diod S 17, 9, 4; pap e.g. BGU 1057, 22 [I b.c.]; POxy 898, 15; PLond 932, 9 [also s. Preis.; Wsd 16:11; Sir 41:1; s. Nägeli 30) not distracted ἀ. διανοίᾳ with undisturbed mind IEph 20:2 (cp. Epict. 3, 22, 69; Diod S 40, 3, 7).—DELG s.v. σπάω.

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Preis. Preis. = FPreisigke, Wörterbuch der griech. Papyrusurkunden—Lists 4, 6

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 101
